title: ActiveCampaign vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign and Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your business best.
intro: ActiveCampaign and Omnisend are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign excels in enterprise-level automation and CRM, while Omnisend specializes in e-commerce with omnichannel capabilities. Choose based on your industry, team size, and required feature set.
choose_a_if: You need advanced CRM and lead scoring capabilities for sales-focused workflows
You want deep automation for complex, multi-step customer journeys
Your business requires site tracking and behavioral trigger-based campaigns
choose_b_if: You're an e-commerce brand looking for easy SMS and email integration
You want to start free with a generous 250-subscriber limit
You need pre-built workflows and a product picker for retail marketing

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign is better for businesses requiring advanced CRM, complex automation, and lead scoring. Omnisend is better for e-commerce brands wanting simplicity and omnichannel features. Both have identical G2 ratings (4.5/5), so the choice depends on your specific use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend is cheaper to start because it offers a free tier for up to 250 subscribers, while ActiveCampaign starts at $15/month with no free option. For paid plans, both start around $15-16/month, but Omnisend's freemium model gives small businesses a zero-cost entry point.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible. Both platforms typically support data export and API integrations. However, you'll need to manually migrate automation workflows and test them in ActiveCampaign, as the platform structures differ. Contact support teams for migration assistance and timeline planning.
